zh.title: 以太坊/美元技术分析：震荡偏弱延续
zh.content:
当前以太坊价格运行在1730下方，整体呈现震荡偏弱格局，短线高点不断受压，趋势略向下延伸，市场情绪谨慎延续调整态势运行特征

技术指标显示价格持续低于20日均线1733附近，均线构成短期压制。MACD柱值1.52维持正值但动能减弱，反映反弹乏力

下方关键支撑位位于1715至1710区间，若跌破可能加速下探1700整数关口，上方阻力1733与1740构成压力带

操作上建议以区间震荡思路为主，未有效站稳1733前不宜追多，可关注回踩1715附近分批低吸，严格止损

en.title: Ethereum vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Ethereum against the US dollar is currently in a mild consolidation phase after a slight 24-hour decline of 0.38%. Price action remains compressed between 1717 and 1734, indicating indecision among participants. The market is trading just below the 20-period SMA at 1733, suggesting short-term bearish pressure. Overall structure leans sideways-to-bearish, with momentum lacking a clear directional breakout and volatility remaining relatively subdued, with no clear breakout catalyst emerging yet.

Momentum indicators present a mixed picture. MACD is slightly positive at 1.52036, hinting at residual bullish momentum, but the lack of a signal line reading limits confirmation of trend strength. With RSI unavailable, broader momentum assessment relies on price structure. The SMA(20) slope appears flattening, reinforcing a neutral-to-bearish bias as upward follow-through continues to stall beneath short-term resistance levels recently, keeping traders cautious in the near term.

Immediate support is located at the recent low of 1717.69, with a break below this level exposing potential downside extension toward the psychological 1700 area. On the upside, resistance is first seen at the SMA(20) near 1733, followed by the intraday high at 1734.34. A decisive breakout above this cluster would be required to shift momentum back toward bullish continuation, as consolidation continues within tight intraday range.

From a trading perspective, the market favors range-bound strategies unless a breakout confirms direction. Buying near support around 1718–1717 with tight risk control may offer favorable reward-to-risk, while short setups near 1733–1734 remain viable on rejection. Traders should wait for confirmation beyond either boundary before committing to trend trades, as false breakouts remain a risk in low-volatility conditions until volatility expands significantly in either direction.
